question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SynchronizationStrategy on the rateLimitBucket (unlike gasLimitBucket which uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED) to race concurrent threads past the tryConsume(1) check, allowing more requests than the configured requestsPerSecond capacity, causing sustained overload of 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED on gasLimitBucket to create a global lock contention bottleneck by sending many concurrent high-gas requests, causing thread starvation that degrades 30%+ of processing node throughput?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on rateLimitBucket — which refills all tokens as fast as possible at the start of each second — to send a full burst of requestsPerSecond (500) requests in the first millisecond of every second, starving all other users for the remaining 999ms and degrading 30%+ of processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on gasLimitBucket — which refills all gasPerSecond tokens at the start of each second — to consume the entire gas budget in a single burst request with maximum gas, blocking all other users from submitting gas-consuming requests for the rest of that second, causing 30%+ processing degradation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket configured with opcodeRequestsPerSecond=1 and no SynchronizationStrategy to race two concurrent requests past tryConsume(1), both succeeding simultaneously, causing the heavy opcode endpoint to process more requests than intended and overload 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket is a single global shared bean (no per-IP, no per-user isolation) to fully consume the global 500 RPS budget from a single connection, denying service to all other users and degrading 30%+ of network processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket is a single global shared bean with no per-user isolation to consume the entire gasPerSecond budget by submitting a single request with the maximum allowed gas limit (15,000,000), blocking all other users' gas-consuming requests for the remainder of that second?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the asymmetric synchronization design — rateLimitBucket has no SynchronizationStrategy while gasLimitBucket uses SYNCHRONIZED — to cause a TOCTOU race where multiple threads pass the rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) check concurrently before any token is deducted, allowing more requests than the configured limit and overloading 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() truncation (Math.floorDiv(gas, 10_000)) to submit requests with gas values just above multiples of 10,000 (e.g., gas=10,001 consumes 1 token, same as gas=19,999) to maximize actual EVM computation per gas token consumed, exhausting CPU on processing nodes while consuming minimal throttle budget?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() zero-return path — where gas <= GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10,000) returns 0 tokens consumed — to submit unlimited requests with gas values of exactly 10,000 that completely bypass the gasLimitBucket throttle, causing unbounded EVM execution load on 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() zero-return for gas=0 to submit requests with gas=0 that consume zero tokens from gasLimitBucket, bypassing gas throttling entirely and allowing unlimited gas-free requests that degrade 30%+ of processing node capacity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) consumes a token even when the subsequent gasLimitBucket check fails — with no token restoration on gas-check failure — to deliberately trigger gas-limit failures that waste rate-limit tokens for legitimate users, causing effective RPS starvation across 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gasLimitRefundPercent=100 default — which restores 100% of gasLimit back to the bucket on failed transactions — to repeatedly submit high-gas requests that intentionally fail (e.g., revert), getting full gas tokens restored each time, effectively bypassing the gas throttle and sustaining high EVM load on 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the restore() path — which calls gasLimitBucket.addTokens(tokens) after a failed transaction — to create a feedback loop where each failed high-gas request restores tokens that allow the next high-gas request, sustaining continuous maximum-gas EVM execution that degrades 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket built without SynchronizationStrategy to race two concurrent opcode requests past the single-token bucket, both consuming the one available token simultaneously due to unsynchronized read-modify-write, causing double the intended opcode processing load on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the capacity=rateLimit and refillGreedy(rateLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) configuration — where capacity equals refill rate — to perform a perfectly timed burst attack that consumes all 500 tokens at second boundary, then immediately consumes the next 500 tokens as they refill, sustaining near-continuous maximum load that degrades 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the capacity=gasLimit and refillGreedy(gasLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) configuration on gasLimitBucket to time requests at second boundaries, consuming the full gas budget twice in rapid succession (once at end of second N, once at start of second N+1), causing a double-burst that overwhelms 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-connection rate limiting in rateLimitBucket to open many parallel HTTP connections and collectively exhaust the global 500 RPS budget, causing all legitimate users to receive ThrottleException responses and degrading 30%+ of processing node throughput?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer truncation in scaleGas(gas) — Math.floorDiv(gas, 10_000) — where gas values 10,001 through 19,999 all consume exactly 1 token, to submit requests with gas=19,999 that consume 1 gas token but execute nearly 2x the EVM computation compared to gas=10,001, amplifying CPU load on 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED on gasLimitBucket — which serializes all gas token operations — to create a thundering herd by sending many concurrent requests that all block on the synchronized lock, causing thread pool exhaustion on 30%+ of processing nodes even before any EVM execution occurs?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of SynchronizationStrategy on rateLimitBucket to cause a race condition where N concurrent threads all call tryConsume(1) simultaneously and all receive true (bucket not yet decremented), allowing N times the intended request rate through and overloading 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the restore() method — which calls gasLimitBucket.addTokens() without any upper-bound check beyond Bucket4j's internal capacity cap — to trigger a scenario where many concurrent failed transactions all call restore() simultaneously, potentially adding tokens beyond the intended capacity due to race conditions in addTokens(), allowing more gas-consuming requests than the throttle should permit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the global singleton rateLimitBucket bean — shared across all application threads with no per-user partitioning — to perform a low-and-slow token exhaustion attack that keeps the bucket perpetually near-empty, causing intermittent ThrottleException for all other users and degrading 30%+ of processing node availability?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket with capacity=1 and refillGreedy(1, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) to permanently occupy the single opcode token by timing requests to arrive exactly when the token refills, preventing any other user from ever accessing the opcode endpoint and degrading that processing path by 100%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the float arithmetic in gasLimitRefundPercent — computed as (long)(gasLimit * gasLimitRefundPercent / 100f) — to cause floating-point precision loss that results in slightly more tokens being restored than consumed, allowing the gas bucket to slowly accumulate excess tokens over many requests and permitting higher-than-intended gas throughput that overloads 30%+ of processing nodes?
